[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held during the {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|sacred boundary|ja|結界}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ}}; Kumogahata, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)}} – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ}}; Kyuta, {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区}} – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区}} – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; {{ill|Miyama Town|ja|美山町 (京都府)}}, {{ill|Nantan City|ja|南丹市}} – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Nataso Village Matsuage|ja|名田庄村}} ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢}}); {{ill|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture|ja|おおい町}}, {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大飯郡}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama City|ja|小浜市}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]]

revision.text:
[[File:広河原松上げ2.jpg|thumb|広河原の松上げ]] [[ファイル:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|雲ケ畑の松上げ（終い火）]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|花脊松上げの柱松]] '''松上げ'''（まつあげ）は[[京都]]～[[丹波国|丹波]]～[[若狭]]（[[鯖街道|若狭街道]]）にかけて[[お盆]]の時期（概ね8月24日の[[地蔵盆]]）に行われる[[火祭り]]で、「松上げ祭り」「松上げ神事」とも呼ばれ、地域によっては'''上げ松'''ともいう。 総体的に山間部に多く伝承されており、[[山火事]]を恐れ、火伏（防火）にご利益がある[[京都市]][[右京区]]の[[愛宕神社]]を崇拝する[[愛宕信仰]]に端を発するとされ、地域によって多少所作の違いはあるが、巨大な[[松明]]に見立てた「柱松（御柱）」という立てた長棒の上部に「笠」という受け籠を取り付け、松明を投げ入れ着火し、最後に柱を倒して終わるのが一般的な形式。柱の周囲に「地松」という松明を灯して[[結界]]としたり、笠に最初に火を点ける「一番点火」を競い合ったり、柱が倒れた後に種火や[[炭]]をもらい受けたりとの違いがある。また、基本的には男性のみの催事で、[[喪|服喪]]中や[[出産]]があった家からは参加不可の決まりもあるが、人手不足から禁忌を解く地域もある[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm 京都北山の松上げと愛宕信仰] 八木 透（『創造する市民』第52号、京都市社会教育振興財団、1997年）。 {{big|松上げ（上げ松）の開催地と開催日}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 京ごよみ（8月の年中行事）] 京都市観光協会
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ 南丹市 上げ松／松上げ] 森の京都博実行委員会
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/category/page.asp?Page=18 松上げ] 小浜市役所
* [[高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|雲ケ畑松上げ]]；京都市[[北区 (京都市)|北区]][[雲ケ畑]]－8月24日 * [[久多#松上げ|久多宮の町松上げ]]；京都市[[左京区]]久多－8月23日 * 花脊の松上げ；京都市左京区花脊八桝町－8月15日（以前は8月24日） * 広河原松上げ：京都市左京区広河原－8月24日 * 小塩の上げ松：京都市右京区[[京北町]]－8月23日に近い土曜日 * 鶴ヶ岡の上げ松；[[南丹市]][[美山町 (京都府)|美山町]]－8月24日 * 盛郷の上げ松；南丹市美山町－8月24日 * 川合の上げ松；南丹市美山町－8月24日 * 殿の上げ松；南丹市美山町－8月24日 * 芦生の上げ松；南丹市美山町－8月24日 * [[牧山の松明]]；南丹市美山町－8月24日 * [[名田庄村]]の松上げ（[[スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|福谷の大火勢]]）；[[福井県]][[大飯郡]][[おおい町]]－8月23・24日 * 小浜の松上げ；福井県[[小浜市]]の[[南川]]河原（滝谷・西相生）－8月23日 [[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）は[[新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行]]のため開催が一部を除いて中止となった。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 映像資料[編集][編集] == 公益財団法人京都市文化観光資源保護財団と京都市文化財保護課による「京都の歴史と文化 映像ライブラリー」において記録映像が公開されている。 * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php 京都の松上げ行事 ー久多・雲ヶ畑ー]　29分24秒 製作年月：昭和64年（1989）1月 製作：京都市文化観光局（企画） 制作：ＫＢＳ京都、アート・プラザ == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=小塩上げ松、３年連続中止　京都の炎の伝統行事、材料不足 |date=20150818090915}} [[京都新聞]]2015年8月18日 {{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まつあけ}} [[Category:日本の年中行事]] [[Category:8月]] [[Category:夏]] [[Category:京都府の祭り]] [[Category:京都府の文化]] [[Category:福井県の祭り]] [[Category:福井県の文化]] [[Category:儀式の火]]

[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/category/page.asp?Page=18 松上げ] 小浜市役所

[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held during the {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|sacred boundary|ja|結界}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

revision.text:
[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} held during the {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆|12=draft}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=draft}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=draft}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=draft}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=draft}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|12=draft}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|sacred boundary|ja|結界|12=draft}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office

Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]]

Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|右京区|" to "|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|"
revision.text:
[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held during the {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|sacred boundary|ja|結界|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; Kumogahata, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|ja|北区 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; Kyuta, {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, {{ill|Ukyo-ku, Kyoto|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; {{ill|Miyama Town|ja|美山町 (京都府)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan City|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Nataso Village Matsuage|ja|名田庄村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}); {{ill|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture|ja|おおい町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大飯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama City|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]]

[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|qq=}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|qq=}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2020|qq=}} ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]]

This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2020|qq=}} ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]]

[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|qq=}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|qq=}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2020|qq=}} ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Fire rituals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[en:Matsuage]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals of Fukui prefecture]]

revision.text:
[[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|qq=}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|qq=}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2020|qq=}} ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Traditional annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese culture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals of Fukui prefecture]]

This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals of Fukui prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|qq=}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|qq=}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

[[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuage]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuage]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|qq=|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|qq=|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|qq=|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|qq=|1=Kumogahata Matsuage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673249}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|qq=|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|qq=|1=Makiyama's Torches|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221872}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|qq=|1=Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313965}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:August]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:松上げ]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}}

[[ja:松上げ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office

[[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association

Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association

[[ja:松上げ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

[[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). revision.text:
[[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

[[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876853]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]], and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] in Kyoto's [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a [[:en:Bai sema|sacred boundary]], competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office
* {{ill|Kumogahata Matsuage|ja|高雲寺 (京都市)#雲ケ畑松上げ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumogahata Matsuage|1=Kumogahata Matsuage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673249}}; Kumogahata, [[:en:Kita-ku, Kyoto|Kita-ku, Kyoto]] – August 24 * {{ill|Kyuta Town Matsuage|ja|久多#松上げ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuta Town Matsuage|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}; Kyuta, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto]] – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; [[:en:Miyama, Kyoto|Miyama Town]], [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan City]] – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|1=Makiyama's Torches|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221872}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nataso Village Matsuage]] ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|1=Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313965}}); [[:en:Ōi, Fukui|Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōi District, Fukui|Ōi District]] – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama City]], Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In [[:en:2020|2020]] ([[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
