Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|Bai sema|en|Bai sema|WD=Q803695|lt=sacred boundary}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association

{{ill|Ōi, Fukui|en|Ōi, Fukui|WD=Q384981|lt=Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōi District, Fukui|en|Ōi District, Fukui|WD=Q384971|lt=Ōi District}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Ōi, Fukui|en|Ōi, Fukui|WD=Q384981|lt=Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōi District, Fukui|en|Ōi District, Fukui|WD=Q384971|lt=Ōi District}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} held during the {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Obon}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|Bai sema|en|Bai sema|WD=Q803695|lt=sacred boundary}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee

Keihoku, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; {{ill|Miyama, Kyoto|en|Miyama, Kyoto|WD=Q6884300|lt=Miyama Town}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan City}} – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|1=Makiyama's Torches|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221872}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nataso Village Matsuage}} ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|1=Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313965}}); {{ill|Ōi, Fukui|en|Ōi, Fukui|WD=Q384981|lt=Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōi District, Fukui|en|Ōi District, Fukui|WD=Q384971|lt=Ōi District}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Ōi, Fukui|en|Ōi, Fukui|WD=Q384981|lt=Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōi District, Fukui|en|Ōi District, Fukui|WD=Q384971|lt=Ōi District}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} held during the {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Obon}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|Bai sema|en|Bai sema|WD=Q803695|lt=sacred boundary}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office

Kyuta, {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} – August 23 * Hanase Matsuage; Hanase Yamasu-chō, Sakyō-ku – August 15 (formerly August 24) * Hirogawara Matsuage; Hirogawara, Sakyō-ku – August 24 * Oshio Agematsu; Keihoku, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} – Saturday closest to August 23 * Tsurugaoka Agematsu; {{ill|Miyama, Kyoto|en|Miyama, Kyoto|WD=Q6884300|lt=Miyama Town}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan City}} – August 24 * Morisato Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Kawai Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Tonō Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * Ashiu Agematsu; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Makiyama's Torches|ja|牧山の松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiyama's Torches|1=Makiyama's Torches|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221872}}; Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nataso Village Matsuage}} ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|1=Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313965}}); {{ill|Ōi, Fukui|en|Ōi, Fukui|WD=Q384981|lt=Ōi Town, Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōi District, Fukui|en|Ōi District, Fukui|WD=Q384971|lt=Ōi District}} – August 23–24 * Obama Matsuage; {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[ja:松上げ]] [[File:Hirogawara Matsuage.jpg|thumb|Hirogawara Matsuage]] [[File:Kumogahata Matsuage 2013-08-24A.JPG|thumb|Kumogahata Matsuage (final flames)]] [[File:Hanase Matsuage Park.jpg|thumb|Hanase Matsuage's pillar pine]] '''Matsuage''' (松上げ) is a traditional {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}} held during the {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Obon|1=Obon}} season (around August 24, corresponding with the {{ill|Jizō Bon|ja|地蔵盆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bon|1=Jizobon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424684}}) in areas spanning {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}, and {{ill|Wakasa Province|ja|若狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|1=Q11616610|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616610}} (along the {{ill|Wakasa Kaidō|ja|鯖街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Kaidō|1=Saba Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11674261}}). Also known as "Matsuage Festival" or "Matsuage Ritual," it is sometimes referred to as '''Agematsu''' in certain regions. This ritual is primarily practiced in mountainous areas and is believed to have originated from {{ill|Atago faith|ja|愛宕信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago faith|1=Atago Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493823}}, which venerates {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} in Kyoto's {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} for fire protection. Despite regional variations, the general practice involves erecting a tall pole called a "pillar pine" (御柱) with a basket-like receptacle ("kasa") at the top. Participants throw torches at the basket to ignite it, concluding the festival by toppling the pillar. Variations include surrounding the pillar with torches as a {{ill|Bai sema|en|Bai sema|WD=Q803695|lt=sacred boundary|1=Bai sema}}, competing for the "first ignition" of the basket, or collecting embers and charcoal after the pillar falls. Traditionally, this is a male-only event, with participation restrictions for those in mourning or from households with recent childbirth. However, some areas have relaxed these taboos due to manpower shortages.[http://www.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/mmc01/yagi-to/matuage.htm Kyoto North Mountain Matsuage and Atago Faith] Tōru Yagi, "Creative Citizens," Issue 52, Kyoto City Social Education Promotion Foundation, 1997. {{big|Matsuage (Agematsu) Locations and Dates}}[http://www.kyokanko.or.jp/kyogoyomi/08august/gyoji-08august/page/6 Kyoto Events Calendar (August)] Kyoto City Tourism Association
[http://morinokyotohaku.jp/festival/1179/ Nantanshi Agematsu / Matsuage] Kyoto Forest Expo Executive Committee
[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category/page.asp?Page=18 Matsuage] Obama City Office

Miyama, Nantan City – August 24 * {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nataso Village Matsuage|1=Natashō, Fukui}} ({{ill|Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|ja|スーパー大火勢#福谷の大火勢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuya's Great Fire Festival|1=Q11313965|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11313965}}); {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama City|1=Obama, Fukui}}, Fukui Prefecture (Minamikawa River, Takidani/Nishiaioi) – August 23 In {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 2), many events were canceled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019th year -)|COVID-19 pandemic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Video Resources == The Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Resource Protection Foundation and Kyoto City Cultural Property Preservation Division have published recordings of Matsuage festivals. * [https://www.kyobunka.or.jp/library/events/157.php Kyoto's Matsuage Festivals – Kyuta and Kumogahata] (29 minutes 24 seconds). Produced in January 1989 by Kyoto City Cultural Tourism Bureau, KBS Kyoto, and Art Plaza. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20150818000067 |title=Oshio Agematsu Canceled for Third Consecutive Year Due to Material Shortage |date=20150818090915}} Kyoto Shimbun, August 18, 2015. {{Event-stub}} {{translated page|ja|松上げ|version=95683045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松上げ]] {{wikidata link|Q24876853}} [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Ceremonial flames]] [[Category:Culture in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Culture in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Festivals in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Summer festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
